refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. It is solution-neutral, specifying what the system must achieve (latency compatible with safe operation) without prescribing how. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize safety as a key requirement. No multiple obligations are present that require splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) is less than or equal to 150 ms, ensuring compliance with safety requirements.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency data transfer
Design a real-time data compression and decompression mechanism to reduce transmission time
Establish a prioritized data flow control to ensure critical control commands have minimal delay
Incorporate local predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
Segment the teleoperation system into modular components with clear interface boundaries to isolate latency sources
selected_dp.selected_dp: Establish a prioritized data flow control to ensure critical control commands have minimal delay

potential_sources: Interdependence between data compression and communication protocol
Tight coupling between predictive control and communication latency assumptions
coupling_check.notes: Selecting prioritized data flow control as the DP supports independence by focusing on logical data prioritization rather than specific technologies. This reduces coupling risks compared to combining compression and predictive control tightly with communication protocols.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Prioritization logic can be implemented without introducing new latency overheads
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining modular boundaries for communication, control, and prediction functions in the IM layer to further reduce coupling and improve maintainability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing network and data flow control within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Data flow for monitoring and control feedback

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control tasks flow through this interface

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection for data transmission

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution manages network and data flow control; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module uses control commands but does not primarily manage data flow control; significant diffusion expected.
Teleoperation depends on prioritized commands but does not control data flow prioritization itself.
Safety Services react to alerts but do not manage data flow control; high diffusion expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP of establishing prioritized data flow control, as it directly manages network and data transmission priorities, ensuring minimal delay for critical control commands.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation if command prioritization logic is partially implemented there.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability may affect containment effectiveness.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment is weak, consider refining the DP to explicitly include network-level prioritization mechanisms to better align with NGHE Solution capabilities, avoiding mixing with higher-level command logic.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of establishing prioritized data flow control logically addresses the FR of supporting teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency ensuring safe remote operation. Prioritizing critical control commands to minimize delay directly supports the latency requirement (p95 ≤ 150 ms) and safety concerns.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module if command prioritization logic is partially implemented there.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and appropriate ownership of the DP. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary IM boundary. However, there is a risk of diffusion if prioritization logic is split with Teleoperation.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions using the Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear as the NGHE Solution module manages network and data flow control, interfacing with Telemetry, Tasking, and Network Link, aligning well with the FR and DP.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to explicitly include network-level prioritization mechanisms to better align with NGHE Solution capabilities and avoid mixing with higher-level command logic in Teleoperation.
Clarify interface responsibilities between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation to prevent diffusion of prioritization logic across modules.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. However, given the high safety criticality and the identified risk of diffusion of prioritization logic between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules, further decomposition of the FR is warranted to explicitly separate concerns and clarify interface responsibilities. This will help mitigate diffusion risks and improve architectural clarity.
next_level_sub_frs: Prioritize critical control commands to minimize latency
Interface management between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules

revise.why: Refine the DP to explicitly include network-level prioritization mechanisms to better align with NGHE Solution capabilities and avoid mixing with higher-level command logic in Teleoperation.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR at a high level.
The NGHE Solution module has strong containment and low diffusion scores, making it suitable as the primary IM boundary.
open_risks: Potential overlap and diffusion of prioritization logic between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
Risk of unclear interface responsibilities leading to implementation inconsistencies.
evidence_needed: Verification results measuring end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions.
Documentation clarifying interface responsibilities between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
